addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- entire-search: Search Entire checkpoint history and transcripts with `entire search --json`. Use proactively when the user asks about previous work, commits, sessions, prompts, or historical context in this repository. (Tools: Bash)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

addedBlocks:
## project
Project management CLI — init, list, doctor, status, lint, sync, register, history, templates, resume. Use project_init to scaffold new projects with AI-powered defaults. Use project_doctor for a full project intelligence report. Use project_list to see all projects across machines. Use project_sync with all=true to register all ~/Projects dirs in TrailBase. Use project_resume to continue interrupted sessions (e.g. after timeout).

attachment.content:
- ax: This skill helps with using the @ax-llm/ax TypeScript library for building LLM applications. Use when the user asks about ax(), ai(), f(), s(), agent(), flow(), AxGen, AxAgent, AxFlow, signatures, streaming, or mentions @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-agent: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent(), child agents, namespaced functions, discovery mode, shared fields, llmQuery(...), RLM code execution, recursionOptions, or agent runtime behavior. For tuning and eval with agent.optimize(...), use ax-agent-optimize.
- ax-agent-optimize: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxAgent tuning and evaluation code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about agent.optimize(...), judgeOptions, eval datasets, optimization targets, saved optimizedProgram artifacts, or recursive optimization guidance.
- ax-ai: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AI provider setup and configuration code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about ai(), providers, models, presets, embeddings, extended thinking, context caching, or mentions OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/Azure/Groq/DeepSeek/Mistral/Cohere/Together/Ollama/HuggingFace/Reka/OpenRouter with @ax-llm/ax.
- ax-flow: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxFlow workflow code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about flow(), AxFlow, workflow orchestration, parallel execution, DAG workflows, conditional routing, map/reduce patterns, or multi-node AI pipelines.
- ax-gen: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxGen code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about ax(), AxGen, generators, forward(), streamingForward(), assertions, field processors, step hooks, self-tuning, or structured outputs.
- ax-gepa: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxGEPA optimization code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about AxGEPA, GEPA, Pareto optimization, multi-objective prompt tuning, reflective prompt evolution, validationExamples, maxMetricCalls, or optimizing a generator, flow, or agent tree.
- ax-learn: This skill helps an LLM generate correct AxLearn code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about self-improving agents, trace-backed learning, feedback-aware updates, or AxLearn modes.
- ax-signature: This skill helps an LLM generate correct DSPy signature code using @ax-llm/ax. Use when the user asks about signatures, s(), f(), field types, string syntax, fluent builder API, validation constraints, or type-safe inputs/outputs.
- hivemind-search: Search the collective hivemind for relevant knowledge, experiences, and skills. Use this when you need information that other agents might have stored, like "how to handle authentication", "best practices for X", or when the user asks "check the hivemind for..."
- hivemind-store: Store knowledge, experiences, or skills to the collective hivemind for other agents to learn from. Use when you've solved a problem, discovered a pattern, learned something valuable, or the user asks to "save this to hivemind" or "remember this for other agents."
- hivemind-vote: Vote on mindchunks to provide feedback on their quality and usefulness. Use this after applying knowledge from the hivemind to indicate whether it was helpful (upvote) or not helpful (downvote).
- arize-admin: Manages Arize users, organizations, spaces, projects, roles, role bindings, resource restrictions, and API keys via the ax CLI. Use for enterprise admin workflows: inviting and offboarding users, onboarding new teams, creating custom roles for SAML/SSO mappings, assigning roles to users, restricting project-level access, and managing service keys for multi-tenant architectures. Covers ax users, ax organizations, ax spaces, ax projects, ax roles, ax role-bindings, and ax api-keys.
- arize-ai-provider-integration: Creates, reads, updates, and deletes Arize AI integrations that store LLM provider credentials used by evaluators and other Arize features. Supports any LLM provider (e.g. OpenAI, Anthropic, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, Vertex AI, Gemini, NVIDIA NIM). Use when the user mentions AI integration, LLM provider credentials, create integration, list integrations, update credentials, delete integration, or connecting an LLM provider to Arize.
- arize-annotation: Creates and manages annotation configs (categorical, continuous, freeform label schemas) and annotation queues (human review workflows) on Arize. Applies human annotations to project spans via the Python SDK. Use when the user mentions annotation config, annotation queue, label schema, human feedback, bulk annotate spans, update_annotations, labeling queue, annotate record, or human review.
- arize-compliance-audit: INVOKE THIS SKILL when auditing an AI agent or LLM app for regulatory compliance. Covers EU AI Act, GPAI Code of Practice, GDPR, NIST AI RMF, Colorado AI Act, HIPAA, and ISO 42001. Scans the codebase for compliance gaps, cross-references Arize instrumentation for audit trail coverage, and produces an actionable remediation checklist tailored to the selected frameworks.
- arize-dataset: Creates, manages, and queries Arize datasets and examples. Covers dataset CRUD, appending examples, exporting data, and file-based dataset creation using the ax CLI. Use when the user needs test data, evaluation examples, or mentions create dataset, list datasets, export dataset, append examples, dataset version, golden dataset, or test set.
- arize-evaluator: Handles LLM-as-judge and code evaluator workflows on Arize including creating/updating evaluators, running evaluations on spans or experiments, managing tasks, trigger-run operations, column mapping, and continuous monitoring. Use when the user mentions create evaluator, LLM judge, code evaluator, hallucination, faithfulness, correctness, relevance, run eval, score spans, score experiment, trigger-run, column mapping, continuous monitoring, or improve evaluator prompt.
- arize-experiment: Creates, runs, and analyzes Arize experiments for evaluating and comparing model performance. Covers experiment CRUD, exporting runs, comparing results, and evaluation workflows using the ax CLI. Use when the user mentions create experiment, run experiment, compare models, model performance, evaluate AI, experiment results, benchmark, A/B test models, or measure accuracy.
- arize-instrumentation: Adds Arize AX tracing to an LLM application for the first time. Follows a two-phase agent-assisted flow to analyze the codebase then implement instrumentation after user confirmation. Use when the user wants to instrument their app, add tracing from scratch, set up LLM observability, integrate OpenTelemetry or openinference, or get started with Arize tracing.
- arize-link: Generates deep links to the Arize UI for traces, spans, sessions, datasets, labeling queues, evaluators, and annotation configs. Produces clickable URLs for sharing Arize resources with team members. Use when the user wants to link to or open a trace, span, session, dataset, evaluator, or annotation config in the Arize UI.
- arize-prompt-optimization: Optimizes, improves, and debugs LLM prompts using production trace data, evaluations, and annotations. Extracts prompts from spans, gathers performance signal, and runs a data-driven optimization loop using the ax CLI. Use when the user mentions optimize prompt, improve prompt, make AI respond better, improve output quality, prompt engineering, prompt tuning, or system prompt improvement.
- arize-prompts: INVOKE THIS SKILL for Arize Prompt Hub and `ax prompts` workflows: author or import templates and save (Workflows A–B), label/promote (C), or list/get/edit/delete/duplicate (D). Use when the user mentions ax prompts, Prompt Hub, creating/editing/saving a prompt, `{variable}` placeholders, or production/staging labels. For improving prompt text using traces or eval scores, use arize-prompt-optimization. For running experiments, use arize-experiment.
- arize-trace: Downloads, exports, and inspects existing Arize traces and spans to understand what an LLM app is doing or debug runtime issues. Covers exporting traces by ID, spans by ID, sessions by ID, and root-cause investigation using the ax CLI. Use when the user wants to look at existing trace data, see what their LLM app is doing, export traces, download spans, investigate errors, or analyze behavior regressions.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud (requires claude.ai account access)). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- claude-in-chrome: Automates your Chrome browser to interact with web pages - clicking elements, filling forms, capturing screenshots, reading console logs, and navigating sites. Opens pages in new tabs within your existing Chrome session. Requires site-level permissions before executing (configured in the extension). - When the user wants to interact with web pages, automate browser tasks, capture screenshots, read console logs, or perform any browser-based actions. Always invoke BEFORE attempting to use any mcp__claude-in-chrome__* tools.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text:
Here's where things stand uncommitted on `main`:

**Arize tracing wired into the LLM path** (`convex/agents.ts`, new `convex/arizeTracing.ts`) — `experimental_telemetry` added to every `generateText`/`runLlm`/`runPlainLlm` call with feature/persona/provider/model metadata, plus `flushArize()` calls after each run so spans actually ship before the Convex action returns.

**`llm-parsing.ts` extracted as its own module** (`src/utils/llm-parsing.ts` + test) — `extractTaggedJson`, `stripTaggedJson`, `parseJudgeOutput`, `clampScore` pulled out of `agents.ts` into a tested, reusable util (used by both the app and the new eval judge).

**A real eval harness under `evals/`** — `judge.ts`, `run-apparatus.ts`, `run-rubric.ts`, `run-robustness.ts`, `run-experiment.ts`, with datasets (`*.jsonl`) and score outputs (`*-scores.json`). This looks like it's exercising the apparatus/rubric/robustness behavior against real model calls and scoring them with an LLM judge.

**Mutation testing added** (`stryker.config.json`) — scoped to three pure/well-tested util modules (`reasoning-tags`, `feature-flags`, `auth-preference`) since Stryker's sandbox copy chokes on the symlinked agent-skill dot-dirs.

**Other untracked pieces**: `src/utils/published-metadata.ts` (118 lines, not yet inspected in depth), `skills-lock.json` (skill source/hash manifest, likely from `npx convex ai-files install` or similar tooling).

**Plus modified files** not yet diffed in detail: `twyne-editor.tsx`, `personas-panel.tsx`, `idb.ts`, route files, `ai-client.ts`, `ai-orchestrator.test.ts`, `background-research.ts`, `desktop-bridge.ts`, `convex-sync.test.ts`, `idb-preferences.test.ts`, `settings/index.tsx`, `types/index.ts`.

Want me to dig into any of those remaining diffs (e.g. the editor/personas-panel changes, or `published-metadata.ts`) for more detail?

+# --- Arize (LLM observability + evals) ---
+# OpenInference tracing for every server-side generateText call in
+# convex/agents.ts, plus a destination for offline experiment scoring.
+# Set ARIZE_API_KEY + ARIZE_SPACE_ID in the Convex deployment env (skate
+# has the values); without them the tracer stays dormant and flushArize()
+# is a no-op. ARIZE_PROJECT_NAME is the Arize project the spans land in
+# and matches the model_id resource attribute (default: twyne).
+# ARIZE_API_KEY=
+# ARIZE_SPACE_ID=
+# ARIZE_PROJECT_NAME=twyne
+# Arize is region-sharded; spans must go to the space's region or they 401.
+# ARIZE_REGION=ca-central-1a            # us-east-1b | us-central-1a | eu-west-1a | ca-central-1a
+# ARIZE_OTLP_ENDPOINT=                  # optional full override of the OTLP URL
+#
+# Offline eval harness (no Arize creds needed — uses the Bifrost vars above):
+#   npm run eval        -> evals/run-experiment.ts: replays evals/dataset.jsonl
+#                          through the real prompt builders, writes evals/runs.json
+#   npm run eval:judge  -> evals/judge.ts: scores runs.json with the two LLM-as-judge
+#                          rubrics (faithfulness-to-draft, feedback-helpfulness),
+#                          writes evals/scores.json. JUDGE_MODEL overrides the judge.
+# JUDGE_MODEL=neuralwatt/kimi-k2.6
+
 # --- Rivet ---
 # Optional: self-hosted or managed Rivet agentOS endpoint.
 # RIVET_ENDPOINT=https://your-rivet-instance.com

content.text:
Here's the full picture of what's staged for the next commit(s):

## 1. Arize observability (`convex/arizeTracing.ts`, `convex/agents.ts`, `.env.example`, `package.json`)
New OpenTelemetry/OpenInference tracer for Convex `"use node"` actions. Dormant unless `ARIZE_API_KEY`+`ARIZE_SPACE_ID` are set (mirrors the existing `posthog.ts` pattern). Uses `OpenInferenceSimpleSpanProcessor` (sync export) instead of batch, since Convex actions are short-lived and would drop spans mid-batch otherwise — every `runLlm`/`runPlainLlm` call now sets `experimental_telemetry` and calls `flushArize()` before returning. Region-sharded endpoint defaults to `ca-central-1a` (matches your memory note that US hosts 401 a CA space).

## 2. `llm-parsing.ts` extraction (`src/utils/llm-parsing.ts` + test)
`extractTaggedJson`, `stripTaggedJson`, `parseJudgeOutput`, `clampScore`, and a new `extractFirstJsonObject` consolidated out of both `convex/agents.ts` and `src/utils/ai-client.ts` (each had its own duplicate copy) into one tested module both now import.

## 3. Apparatus research providers (`types/index.ts`, `idb.ts`, `background-research.ts`, `ai-client.ts`, `settings/index.tsx`, `apparatus/index.tsx`)
New `ApparatusSettings.researchProvider`: `hosted | tinyfish | model-web-search | web-mcp`. Background research can now run client-side instead of only the hosted Convex action:
- **tinyfish** — browser calls TinyFish's search API directly with a user-supplied key
- **model-web-search** — new `AiFeature: "research-web-search"`, routes through the user's configured BYOK model (new `runClientResearchWebSearch` in `ai-client.ts`)
- **web-mcp** — JSON-RPC `tools/call` against a user-given MCP endpoint, with fairly defensive response unwrapping (`normalizeMcpSources` digs through `result.content[].text` JSON-in-text payloads)

All three fall back to the hosted path if they return nothing. Settings UI in `settings/index.tsx` got a new "Research provider" section with conditional fields per provider.

## 4. Inline persona reply thread (`twyne-editor.tsx`, `personas-panel.tsx`)
The note popover in the editor now shows a live, scrollable reply thread instead of closing on reply. Wired via a `window` event bus: editor requests a thread (`twyne:request-persona-thread`) → panel responds (`twyne:persona-reply-thread`), plus `twyne:persona-replying` / `twyne:persona-reply-error` events so the popover shows in-flight/error state. Also softened a Pro-plan-specific error message to "signed-in Twyne account."

## 5. Published-page SEO (`published-metadata.ts`, `[handle]/[slug]/index.tsx`, `blog/[slug]/index.tsx`)
New shared util fetches the published piece server-side via `routeLoader$` (was previously client-only `useVisibleTask$`, so crawlers/social previews got generic meta tags). Real `og:description`/`twitter:description`/author meta now derived from the piece content, with HTML-stripping + 180-char truncation. Client-side task now skips re-fetching if the loader already has the piece.

## 6. Mutation testing (`stryker.config.json`)
Scoped to three pure modules with existing 1:1 tests (`reasoning-tags`, `feature-flags`, `auth-preference`); ignores the symlinked agent-skill dot-dirs that broke Stryker's sandbox copy.

## 7. `evals/` harness (untracked, gitignored outputs)
`run-experiment.ts`, `run-rubric.ts`, `run-apparatus.ts`, `run-robustness.ts`, `judge.ts` — replays datasets through the real prompt builders and scores with an LLM judge, all wired as `npm run eval*` scripts.

## Minor fixes
- `desktop-bridge.ts`: guards `!window.location` in addition to `typeof window === "undefined"`
- `background-research.ts`: `kickBackgroundResearch`/`onDraftChanged` no longer bail when there's no `activeClient` (needed for client-side-only research providers)
- Test files updated to cover the new `ApparatusSettings` fields and add proper `afterAll` cleanup for `mock.restore()` / global shims

Nothing is committed yet — this is all working-tree state on `main`. Want me to stage/commit any of this, or keep going (e.g. check `convex/agents.ts`'s remaining diff, or run the test suite)?

## A) `conversational-interview.tsx` and `anti-tabula-rasa.tsx`

**File:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/components/onboarding/conversational-interview.tsx`

Props interface (lines 65–71):
```ts
interface ConversationalInterviewProps {
  mode: "first-run" | "refine";
  initialBrief?: ProjectBrief;
  onComplete$: PropFunction<(brief: ProjectInterviewAnswers) => void>;
  onCancel$?: PropFunction<() => void>;
  cancelLabel?: string;
}
```
- No `initialAnswers` prop here — only `initialBrief?: ProjectBrief` (whole brief, not raw answers).
- Internal `ComponentStore` (lines 80–90): `messages`, `loading`, `error`, `synthesis: Synthesis | null`, `liveDraft: InterviewDossierDraft | null`, `editingField`, `draft`, `initialized`.
- Structure: chat thread driven by `runTurn$` (line 167) which calls `loadAiSettingsFromIdb()` (line 176) then either `runClientInterviewTurn` (BYOK path, line 181) or a Convex action `api.agents.runInterviewTurn` (server fallback, line 198–205). On `result.kind === "question"` it appends a chat message; on synthesis it sets `store.synthesis`. `onComplete$` fires only when the writer clicks "Take it to the desk" (`acceptSynthesis`, line 271–274), passing `store.synthesis.brief` (a `ProjectInterviewAnswers`).
- No existing inline "settings" step. The only settings touchpoint is reading AI settings via `loadAiSettingsFromIdb` at the top of `runTurn` to decide BYOK vs server — this is a natural point to also check/prompt for AI provider configuration before letting the first turn run, since `hasConfiguredAiProvider(settings)` is already computed at line 178.

**File:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/components/onboarding/anti-tabula-rasa.tsx`

Props interface (lines 107–119):
```ts
interface AntiTabulaRasaProps {
  initialAnswers?: ProjectInterviewAnswers | null;
  mode?: InterviewMode; // "first-run" | "refine", default "first-run"
  onSubmit$?: PropFunction<
    (
      answers: ProjectInterviewAnswers,
      existingMaterial?: string,
      filename?: string,
    ) => void
  >;
  onCancel$?: PropFunction<() => void>;
}
```
- Internal structure: a fixed array `STEPS: InterviewStep[]` (lines 23–105) — 8 steps (`workingTitle`, `format`, `audience`, `goal`, `tone`, `constraints`, `successSignal`, plus a final `kind: "import"` step with no `field` for pasting/uploading prior material).
- `store.answers` seeded from `DEFAULT_INTERVIEW_ANSWERS` merged with `initialAnswers` (lines 128–138, imported from `../../utils/anti-tabula-rasa`).
- `goNext$` (line 144) advances `store.step`; on the last step it calls `onSubmit$(store.answers, store.existingMaterial, store.importedFilename)` if provided, else falls back to a legacy `window.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("twyne:submit-interview", ...))` (lines 153–177).
- The `import` step (`STEPS[7]`, kind `"import"`) is the only non-answer step today — a textarea plus a hidden `<input type="file">` (lines 423–493) for `.txt/.md/.html`. **This is the natural slot pattern to copy for a new "configure your AI provider / interview style" step**: add a new `StepKind` (e.g. `"settings"`) and a `InterviewStep` entry, since the component already supports non-field steps that don't write into `store.answers`.

## B) `auth-panel.tsx` and `signin/index.tsx`

**File:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/components/auth/auth-panel.tsx` (full file read, 850 lines).

- `AuthPanel` takes **no props at all** (`component$(() => {...})`, line 36) — no `onSuccess`/`onSignedIn` callback, and it does not emit any custom event on successful sign-in (unlike `AntiTabulaRasa`'s legacy `twyne:submit-interview` event). It is purely self-contained: it reads `useAuth()` and renders based on `auth.value`.
- `auth.value.user` becomes truthy via the `AuthContext`/`AuthProvider` in `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/utils/auth-context.tsx`. `AuthProvider` (lines 41–80+) runs a `useVisibleTask$` that: (1) tries ATProto session restore via `initSession()` from `./atproto` — if present, sets `authState.value = { user: {...}, loading: false, provider: "atproto" }` (lines 56–66); else (2) falls back to `authClient.useSession` (better-auth) and syncs from that atom (`syncFromAtom`, starting line 80). `AuthPanel`'s own internal OTP/passkey flows just call `authClient.signIn...`/`emailOtp...`; the actual `auth.value.user` flip happens reactively once the auth-client session settles (the `useVisibleTask$` in `AuthPanel` at lines 296–306 watches `auth.value.user` to trigger the post-OTP "Add a passkey?" offer, confirming `auth.value` is the single source of truth observed externally).
- Because there is no callback prop, any caller that wants to react to "just signed in" must do so the same way `auth/callback/index.tsx` and `routes/onboarding/index.tsx` already do: wrap `<AuthPanel />` in a parent that itself calls `useAuth()` and runs a `useVisibleTask$({ track })` on `auth.value.user`/`auth.value.loading` (see `auth/callback/index.tsx` lines 22–46, and `onboarding/index.tsx` lines 94–99 which checks `auth.value.user` after `briefDone.value` flips). This is the pattern to replicate for "post-sign-in next step" UI.

**File:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/routes/signin/index.tsx` (98 lines, full file read).
- It does **not** read or react to auth state at all. It's a static layout (`component$`) that renders header/hero copy and `<AuthPanel />` inside an `<aside>` (line 91). No `useAuth()` import, no `useVisibleTask$`, no redirect-on-success logic. Whatever "offer next step after sign-in" behavior is wanted will need to be added to this route (or into `AuthPanel` itself via a new prop) since today sign-in success on `/signin/` produces no navigation or follow-up UI — the writer is just left looking at the signed-in `AuthPanel` state (`SignedInHeader`, lines 825–849 of auth-panel.tsx).

## D) `loadProjectBrief` — definition and call sites

**Definition:** `/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne/src/utils/anti-tabula-rasa.ts:31-39`
```ts
export function loadProjectBrief(): ProjectBrief | null {
  if (typeof window === "undefined") return null;
  try {
    const raw = localStorage.getItem(BRIEF_STORAGE_KEY);
    return raw ? (JSON.parse(raw) as ProjectBrief) : null;
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}
```
Synchronous, reads `localStorage["twyne-project-brief"]` (`BRIEF_STORAGE_KEY`, line 5). Companion: `saveProjectBrief(brief: ProjectBrief): void` (lines 41–51) writes to localStorage and also persists to the Lix file store (`writeFileAsJson(BRIEF_PATH, brief)`) then calls `markSyncDirty()`.

Call sites (`grep -rln loadProjectBrief`):
- `src/routes/refining/index.tsx:24,62` — `store.brief = loadProjectBrief();` inside `useVisibleTask$`.

## F) Multi-attachment / link-with-note support for dossier creation

**How the starter/imported draft is seeded today** — `src/routes/onboarding/index.tsx`, function `completeOnboarding$` (lines 58–78):
```ts
const completeOnboarding$ = $(
  (answers: ProjectInterviewAnswers, existingMaterial?: string, filename?: string) => {
    const brief = createProjectBrief(answers, null);
    saveProjectBrief(brief);

    if (!loadDraftHtml().trim()) {
      const material = existingMaterial?.trim();
      saveDraftHtml(
        material
          ? buildImportedMaterialDocument(answers, material, filename)

);
```

- `existingMaterial`/`filename` come from `AntiTabulaRasa`'s single `kind: "import"` step (the last `STEPS` entry, `anti-tabula-rasa.tsx` lines 95–104, 423–493) — one big textarea plus one hidden `<input type="file" accept=".txt,.md,.markdown,.html,.htm,...">` whose `onChange$` (`handleFile`, lines 182–190) reads `file.text()` into `store.existingMaterial`. **It is a single document, mutually exclusive with manual paste (uploading replaces the textarea content), no links, no per-item notes, no list of multiple items.**

# Dossier onboarding: route rename, post-signin direction, inline settings, multi-attachment references, prompt wiring

## Context

Today, sign-in leaves the writer with no next step (`/signin/` just shows a "signed in" header with nothing to click), the create-vs-refine route naming is asymmetric (`/onboarding/` vs `/refining/`), settings the writer actually cares about at dossier-creation time (interview style, AI provider, citation defaults) live only on a separate `/settings/` page, the dossier can only absorb a single pasted/uploaded starter document, and none of the agent prompts (persona room, rubric, synthesis) ever see anything beyond the 7 brief-interview answers — bibliography/reference material is invisible to the AI. This plan fixes all four in one pass since they touch the same files (the onboarding route and `ProjectBrief`).

## 1. Route rename: `/onboarding/` → `/dossier/create/`, `/refining/` → `/dossier/refine/`

Nested Qwik City routes (mirrors the existing `src/routes/auth/callback/` pattern), confirmed with the user.

- `git mv src/routes/onboarding/index.tsx src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx`
- `git mv src/routes/refining/index.tsx src/routes/dossier/refine/index.tsx`
- Leave `src/components/onboarding/*` (the component directory) where it is — only routes move, not components.
- Update every call site found by research (exact strings to replace `/onboarding/` → `/dossier/create/`, `/refining/` → `/dossier/refine/`):
  - `src/routes/landings/index.tsx:26`
  - `src/routes/editor/index.tsx:127-128` (comment), `165`, `489`, `718`, `794`
  - `src/routes/index.tsx:18` (comment), `39`, `44`
  - `src/routes/dossier/refine/index.tsx` (post-move path) — the `<Link href="/onboarding/">` at old line 172
  - `src/routes/auth/callback/index.tsx:8`, `17`, `34` (also see §2 — this destination logic changes anyway)
  - `src/utils/byok-feature-coverage.test.ts:30` — path string must become `src/routes/dossier/refine/index.tsx`
  - `e2e/journey.e2e.ts:31` — URL regex `/\/onboarding\//` → `/\/dossier\/create\//`
  - `public/robots.txt:17,20` — `Disallow: /onboarding` / `Disallow: /refining` → `/dossier/create` / `/dossier/refine`

## 2. Post-signin direction

`auth-panel.tsx` has no callback/event on success and `/signin/index.tsx` never reads auth state — that's the actual bug. Fix at the route level, not inside `AuthPanel` (keep it a dumb, reusable component):

- **`src/routes/signin/index.tsx`**: add `useAuth()` + a `useVisibleTask$({ track })` on `auth.value.user`/`auth.value.loading`, following the exact pattern already used in `src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx` (ex-`onboarding/index.tsx` lines 94-99) and `src/routes/auth/callback/index.tsx` (lines 22-46). Once signed in, replace the `<AuthPanel/>` panel with a "You're in — what's next?" card offering two explicit CTAs (no silent auto-redirect, since this is a deliberate landing, not a transient OAuth callback):
  - "Start your dossier" → `/dossier/create/` (always shown)
  - "Go to your desk" → `/editor/` (only shown if `loadProjectBrief() !== null`, via the existing sync helper in `src/utils/anti-tabula-rasa.ts`)
- **`src/routes/auth/callback/index.tsx`**: change `destination` fallback from `/onboarding/` to `/dossier/create/` (line 8, 17, 34) — this route's existing short-delay auto-redirect logic is fine as-is, just the URL changes.

## 3. Lightweight settings inline in dossier-create, link out for the rest

Per your call: inline only what matters at creation time, link to `/settings/` for full provider credential management.

- In `src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx`, add a `store.phase: "setup" | "interview" | "done"` state machine. New **"setup" phase** (shown first, before either `AntiTabulaRasa`/`ConversationalInterview` renders) is a small card with:
  - **Interview style** toggle (Form / Conversation) — reuses `loadWriterSettingsFromIdb`/`saveWriterSettingsToIdb` (`src/utils/idb.ts:404,428`), the exact same persistence the route already does today.
  - **Default citation style** (MLA/APA/Chicago) dropdown — reuses `loadApparatusSettingsFromIdb`/`saveApparatusSettingsToIdb` (`src/utils/idb.ts:452,476`).
  - **BYOK on/off** toggle — reuses `loadAiSettingsFromIdb`/`saveAiSettingsToIdb` (`src/utils/idb.ts:615,638`), just flips `advancedMode`; no per-provider credential form inline.
  - A `<Link href="/settings/">Configure AI providers & Apparatus in full →</Link>` for anything deeper.
  - "Begin" button moves `store.phase = "interview"`, which renders the existing conditional (`AntiTabulaRasa` vs `ConversationalInterview`) exactly as today.
- This is new markup local to the route file — no changes needed to `settings/index.tsx` itself, no shared-component extraction (avoids the duplication risk of the "full inline" option).

## 4. Multi-document/link attachments with a one-line "why"

No existing data model fits (`BibEntry` is per-folio Lix storage, mismatched with the global, pre-folio `ProjectBrief`). Add attachments directly to the brief.

- **`src/types/index.ts`**: add
  ```ts
  export interface DossierAttachment {
    id: string;
    kind: "document" | "link";
    title: string;
    url?: string;      // kind === "link" (or optional source URL for a document)
    text?: string;      // kind === "document", capped ~2000 chars
    why: string;        // required one-liner
    addedAt: number;
  }
  ```

Picking up work on the twyne repo (/home/diogenes/Projects/twyne). Plan file: /home/diogenes/.claude/plans/curried-tickling-comet.md

## Done so far
1. Route rename: `src/routes/onboarding/` → `src/routes/dossier/create/`, `src/routes/refining/` → `src/routes/dossier/refine/` (nested Qwik City routes). All call sites updated: landings/index.tsx, editor/index.tsx (comment + 4 nav() calls), routes/index.tsx, auth/callback/index.tsx (destination type + redirect logic), src/utils/byok-feature-coverage.test.ts (path assertion), e2e/journey.e2e.ts (URL regex), public/robots.txt (Disallow rules). Relative imports inside the two moved route files were fixed for the extra nesting depth (`../../` → `../../../`).
2. Post-signin direction: `src/routes/signin/index.tsx` now tracks `auth.value.user` via useVisibleTask$ and, once signed in, swaps `<AuthPanel/>` for a "What's next?" card with "Start your dossier" (→ /dossier/create/) and, if `loadProjectBrief()` exists, "Go to your desk" (→ /editor/). `auth/callback/index.tsx` destination updated to `/dossier/create/`.
3. Lightweight inline settings in `src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx`: added a `store.phase: "setup" | "interview"` state machine. New "setup" phase (shown first) lets the writer pick Interview style (form/conversational), default citation style (MLA/APA/Chicago), and a BYOK on/off toggle — persisted via the existing `loadWriterSettingsFromIdb`/`saveWriterSettingsToIdb`, `loadApparatusSettingsFromIdb`/`saveApparatusSettingsToIdb`, `loadAiSettingsFromIdb`/`saveAiSettingsToIdb` helpers in `src/utils/idb.ts`. Includes a link to `/settings/` for full provider credential config. "Begin" button moves to phase "interview" which renders the existing AntiTabulaRasa/ConversationalInterview branch unchanged.
4. New `DossierAttachment` type added to `src/types/index.ts` (`{ id, kind: "document"|"link", title, url?, text?, why, addedAt }`), and `ProjectBrief` extended with `attachments: DossierAttachment[]`. `createProjectBrief()` in `src/utils/anti-tabula-rasa.ts` now accepts/merges an optional `attachments` param (3rd arg).
5. New shared component `src/components/onboarding/dossier-attachments-editor.tsx` — add/list/remove UI for attachments (document paste/upload capped at 2000 chars, or link + required one-line "why"). Reuses the codebase's existing hidden-`<input type="file">` + `file.text()` pattern (no FileReader).
6. Wired into `AntiTabulaRasa` (`src/components/onboarding/anti-tabula-rasa.tsx`): new step IX "Dept. of References" (`kind: "attachments"`) renders `<DossierAttachmentsEditor>`; `AntiTabulaRasaProps.onSubmit$` widened to `(answers, existingMaterial?, filename?, attachments?)`; legacy `twyne:submit-interview` event also carries `attachments` now. `src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx`'s `completeOnboarding$` updated to accept the 4th `attachments` param and pass it into `createProjectBrief`.

## In progress / next steps (not yet done)
- **`ConversationalInterview`** (`src/components/onboarding/conversational-interview.tsx`): imports for `DossierAttachment` and `DossierAttachmentsEditor` are already added (lines ~17-25), but NOT yet wired in. Still needs:
  - Add `attachments: DossierAttachment[]` to `ComponentStore` (interface at line ~85), initialized from `props.initialAttachments` (new prop, not yet added).
  - Widen `ConversationalInterviewProps` (line 70-76): add `initialAttachments?: DossierAttachment[]` and change `onComplete$: PropFunction<(brief: ProjectInterviewAnswers) => void>` to also pass attachments, e.g. `(brief, attachments?) => void`.
  - Render `<DossierAttachmentsEditor>` inline on the synthesis-review screen (near line 402-517, before/alongside the "Take it to the desk" button at line 517).
  - Update `acceptSynthesis` (line 276-280) to call `props.onComplete$(store.synthesis.brief, store.attachments)`.
  - `src/routes/dossier/create/index.tsx`'s `<ConversationalInterview onComplete$={completeOnboarding$} .../>` (line ~332) — `completeOnboarding$` signature is `(answers, existingMaterial?, filename?, attachments?)`, so the conversational path needs to call it as `completeOnboarding$(answers, undefined, undefined, attachments)` — may need a small wrapper since `onComplete$` prop type mismatch (2-arg vs 4-arg) will need reconciling.
  - Also wire `initialAttachments` prop through from `dossier/create` (always null/undefined on first-run) and especially `dossier/refine/index.tsx` (should pass `store.brief.attachments` so refine mode can edit existing attachments) for both `AntiTabulaRasa` and `ConversationalInterview` usages in both route files.

- **Task #6 (not started): Serialize brief attachments into agent prompts.**
  - `convex/agentPrompts.ts`: add an `attachmentsBlock(brief)` helper, splice into `buildUserPrompt` (~line 141, after the existing `briefBlock`), `generateLocalFeedback` (~line 220), `buildSynthesisPrompt` (~line 286), `buildRubricReviewPrompt` (~line 307). Cap ~500 chars/item, ~2000 chars total across all attachments in the prompt (separate, tighter cap than the editor's 2000-char-per-item storage cap).
  - `convex/agents.ts`: grep for the `v.object(...)` validator(s) shaping the `brief` arg on action endpoints (action handlers construct `const req: AgentRequest = {...}` around lines 586, 826, 880) — add `attachments` to the validator schema so Convex doesn't reject the new field. No client call-site changes needed beyond what's already done, since `brief: brief ?? null` already carries the field once `ProjectBrief` itself is widened.

- **Task #7 (not started): Verify.**
  - `npx tsc --noEmit` (has been passing cleanly after each prior step — note: occasionally threw spurious/stale JSX-type diagnostics on first read after an edit that cleared on rerun, not real errors, just rerun to confirm).
  - `bun test src/utils/anti-tabula-rasa.test.ts src/utils/byok-feature-coverage.test.ts` and any other onboarding/refine-adjacent unit tests.
  - Manual walkthrough via the `/run` skill: sign in fresh on `/signin/` → confirm CTAs → `/dossier/create/` → setup phase persists choices (verify via `/settings/`) → add attachments in both Form and Conversational modes → complete → confirm `/dossier/refine/` shows saved attachments for editing.
  - Trigger a persona reply / rubric review after completing a dossier with attachments and confirm the model's response actually reflects the attached material.
  - Rerun `e2e/journey.e2e.ts` for the new `/dossier/create/` URL assertion.

Task list IDs in this session: #1-3 done, #4 in_progress (attachments editor — component done, ConversationalInterview wiring remains), #5 pending (same), #6-7 pending.
